En: Sure, why not? Everyone knows politicians are corrupt, and what's more corrupt than letting your entitled spawn run around offing people?
Zh: 当然了，为什么不呢？人人都知道政治家是腐败的，还有什么能比让你的孩子到处瞎跑更腐败的呢？

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: It makes sense: Junkies and art collectors both need a steady stream of income, and what better way to generate income than... murdering a couple of people.
Zh: 有道理：毒品贩子和艺术收藏家都需要稳定的收入来源。那么，要想产生收入，还有什么能比……杀人更好的方法呢。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Sounds good. You can't trust the fuzz, after all. They *say* they've got your back, but do they *really?* More like they've got a *knife* in your back.
Zh: 听起来不错。毕竟你不能相信那些杂音。他们*说*会在背后支撑你，但他们*真的*这么做了吗？他们更像是在你背后*插刀*才对吧。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Sure, yes. They died of an over-abundance of love. Just loved each other to death. Couldn't get enough love. And now they're dead.
Zh: 当然了，没错。他们死于过于沉重的爱。把对方爱到死。永远也要不够的爱。所以现在他们都死了。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Okay, sure. A defunct political ideology came and murdered a couple of people. History repeats itself, and the whole cycle starts again...
Zh: 好吧，当然了。一个不复存在的政治意识形态来了，然后杀了几个人。历史重演，整个循环又重新开始……

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Doesn't matter. The choice has been made. You caught the criminal -- It was Dick Mullen all along. Who's the real detective now? That's right, it's you. Rest easy tonight, *real detective*.
Zh: 没关系。反正已经做出选择了。你抓住了罪犯——一直以来都是迪克·马伦。看看现在谁才是真正的警探？没错，就是你。今晚可以高枕无忧了，*真警探*先生。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Way to go, public corruption cop. You've seen first-hand how the powerful tip the scales of justice in their favour, but today you tipped them back *just a little*. You can rest easy tonight.
Zh: 就该这么做，公共腐败警察。你已经亲眼看见那些有权有势的人是如何让正义的天平朝他们倾斜的，但是今天你把它稍微歪回来了*一点*。今晚你可以睡个好觉了。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Well done, ace detective. You put a dangerous junkie *and* art collector behind bars -- two birds with one stone! Now put your feet up and take a breather.
Zh: 干得好，王牌警探。你抓住了一个危险的毒品贩子，*还有*一个艺术收藏家——这是一石二鸟啊！现在高高抬起你的双脚，好好松一口气吧。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: You've seen it yourself, haven't you? Maybe that cuts a little close to the bone, *jaded cop*. Take it easy -- Don't let the memory of old betrayals haunt you tonight.
Zh: 你亲眼见过了，不是吗？也许那道伤口离骨头稍微近了点，*疲惫警察*。放松点——今晚别让昔日的背叛记忆困扰你了。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Why not? Love gets a lot of people killed... Speaking of, aren't you a little close to the edge, yourself, lover-cop? Better lay off that love stuff, if you can.
Zh: 为什么不呢？爱情让很多人为此丧命……说到这里，你自己是不是也有点太靠近边缘了，爱情警探？如果可以的话，最好还是放下爱情这玩意吧。

ConvTitle: PLAZA ORB / mullen mistaken identity conclusion
En: Case closed, and with no time to spare. Ideologies are dangerous, but communism won't be troubling the people anymore -- all thanks to you, politico-detective.
Zh: 案子破了，一定要争分夺秒。意识形态很危险，但是康米主义再也不会让人们烦恼了——一切多亏了你，政治警探。

En: Awful lonely here. No ruckus. No old men playing their little ball game.
Zh: 这里只有可怕的孤寂。没有喧闹。没有玩小球游戏的老人。

En: Where did the old guys go?
Zh: “那帮老家伙去哪了？”

En: Yeah, where did the old guys go?
Zh: 是啊，那些老家伙去哪了？

En: That's just how craters are. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 弹坑不就是这样的吗。（抛弃思维。）

En: You mean the guys who were playing pétanque?
Zh: ”你是说玩地掷球的那些人？”

En: The pétanque players?
Zh: 地掷球玩家？

En: Yeah, where did they go?
Zh: “是啊，他们去哪了？”

En: Yeah, where did they go?
Zh: 是啊，他们去哪了？

En: Have you noticed people sometimes appear and disappear in places? It's called *doing things*.
Zh: “你有没有注意到有时候人们会在某些地方出现或者消失？这就叫*做事*。”

En: Home? To work? To get something from somewhere? Who knows what people are doing when we're not looking.
Zh: 回家了？去工作了？去别处拿东西了？谁知道我们没注意的时候大家都在干什么。

En: Maybe they too died during the mercenary tribunal?
Zh: 也许他们也死在雇佣兵宣判中了？

En: Wait, maybe they died during the mercenary tribunal?
Zh: “等等，也许他们在雇佣兵宣判的时候死了？”

En: But what if I wanted to talk to them?
Zh: “但是，如果我想跟他们说话呢？”

En: Right. Time to move. [Leave.]
Zh: 没错。该走了。[离开。]

En: Right. Let's move. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧。我们走。”[离开。]

En: It's very unlikely. Everyone scattered once the tribunal started. They're probably fine.
Zh: 不太可能。宣判刚开始每个人都吓跑了。他们可能没什么事。

En: They didn't, the lieutenant calms you. "Trust me. I saw the bodies."
Zh: “他们没有，”警督的话让你平静下来。“相信我。我看过尸体了。”

En: You'll get them next time, the lieutenant replies, looking at the empty crater.
Zh: “下次再找他们吧，”警督看着空荡荡的弹坑回复到。

En: You'll get them next time. But let's be honest, they weren't even in the suspects list.
Zh: 下次再找他们吧。不过说实在的，他们都不在嫌疑人名单上。

En: And, I mean... it would be one twist too many if it turned out these two old men were our killers. That's just unlikely. No... He seems pleased with himself. "We would only have wasted our time here."
Zh: “还有，我是想说……如果这两个老人是我们要找的凶手，那就太过分了。那是不可能的。不会的……”他似乎对自己很满意。“我们只会在这里浪费时间。”

En: You would have wasted *so* much time talking to these two pensioners. Precious case-time.
Zh: 跟这两个领退休金的人聊天会浪费你*太多*时间的。宝贵的查案时间。

En: He's probably right.
Zh: 他也许是对的。

En: Damn. Looks like I missed asking these guys about the dried May bells.\n
Zh: 该死。看来我错过问他们枯萎铃兰的机会了。

En: Damn, I missed asking these guys about the dried May bells.
Zh: “该死，我没问那些人干铃兰的事。”

En: No need to beat yourself down over it. It probably wasn't the definitive piece of the puzzle. Still, somehow it feels like May bells matter somehow.
Zh: 没必要过分责怪自己。它可能不是谜题的最后一片答案。不过，不知道为什么，铃兰就是感觉很重要。

En: Don't beat yourself up about it. I think I gave you a pretty exhaustive answer about those flowers and what they mean.
Zh: “不要因此责怪自己。关于那些花和它们的含义，我想你已经给出了一个十分详尽的答案。”

En: Enough with the goddamn flowers already! Are you a cop or a florist?
Zh: 别再管那些该死的花了！你是警察还是花匠啊？

En: It was probably nothing anyway. Just a little thought you had.
Zh: 反正可能也没什么。只是你自己的一个小想法。

En: *Still* you can't shake the feeling that somehow these may bell do actually matter. If only a little bit.
Zh: *不过*，你还是无法摆脱这些铃兰的确很重要的感觉。只有一点点就好了。

En: The lieutenant is thinking of all the time you've spared. He likes it.
Zh: 警督想的是你所有省下来的时间。他喜欢这样。

En: I am here... Please, listen... It will be hard to hear me…
Zh: 我在这里……请好好听……要想听到我的声音并不容易……

En: I hear you! Please, talk to me! What do you want?
Zh: 我听见了！拜托你，跟我说话吧！你想要什么？

En: Who are you?
Zh: 你是谁？

En: Oh, no. No, no. Whatever you are, I don't want you in my head.
Zh: 哦，不不不。不管你是谁，我不想你出现在我的脑子里。

En: I am so glad… I have no time... The place of many words, before the one so much like yourself... Try to hear me there...
Zh: 我很高兴……我没有时间了……在这个千言万语的地方，在一个很像你的人面前……尝试寻找我的声音吧……

En: I am what has always been here for you… Please... The place of many words, before the one so much like yourself... Try to hear me there...
Zh: 我就是一直在这里等着你的人……请你……在这个千言万语的地方，在一个很像你的人面前……尝试寻找我的声音吧……

En: Forgive me... I have no other way to reach you... Please -- the place of many words, before the one so much like yourself... Try to hear me there...
Zh: 原谅我……要想找到你，我别无他法……请求你——在这个千言万语的地方，在一个很像你的人面前……尝试寻找我的声音吧……

En: All around you, rain falls on the great city of Revachol. Rain drips from the eaves and floods the gutters, washing the filth away.
Zh: 淅淅沥沥的雨水坠落在伟大的瑞瓦肖城。雨滴从屋檐滑落，淹没了水沟，把脏东西冲刷得一干二净。

En: What am I doing?
Zh: 我在做什么？

En: Shake the shivers off. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 抖掉身上的寒颤。[放弃思维。]

En: The spring thaw must be here. The snow is melting...
Zh: 一定是到了春季解冻的时间。雪花正在融化……

En: Looking up at the sky, cold water dripping from your hat.
Zh: 抬头望向天空，冰冷的雨水从你的帽子上滴落。

En: Looking up at the sky, cold water dripping from your hair.
Zh: 抬头望向天空，冰冷的雨水从你的头发上滴落。

En: What do I see?
Zh: 我看见什么了？

En: Grey sky like great battleships, clouds colliding with one another. Rain falls down on the world.
Zh: 像大战舰一样的灰色天空，白色的云朵互相碰撞着。雨点降落在这个世界上。

En: How does it feel?
Zh: 感觉怎么样？

En: Your shirt sticks to your chest. The shoulders of your disco blazer grow heavy. The cold finds its way in under your skin. You shiver, and the city shivers with you.
Zh: 你的衬衫紧贴在胸膛。迪斯科夹克肩部的位置变得沉重起来。寒冷一路钻到你的皮肤之下。你颤抖着，整个城市也跟着你一起颤抖。

En: You're not dressed for this weather. You should get an overcoat, or a patrol cloak.
Zh: 你身上的衣服并不适合这样的天气。你应该找一件大衣，或者巡逻斗篷。

En: What's in the north?
Zh: 北边有什么？

En: What's in the east?
Zh: 东边有什么？

En: What's in the south?
Zh: 南边有什么？

En: What is in the west?
Zh: 西边有什么？

En: There's a Frittte on the corner. A plastic coat is folded into a small triangle under the counter by the window. Beyond that, the strikebreakers have gathered.
Zh: 街角有一间弗利多便利店。一件叠成小三角的塑料衣堆放在窗边的柜台下面。再往前，就是工贼的聚集地了。

En: The great gates of the industrial harbour are locked. A chill runs down your back. You shudder like an animal trying to shake water from its hide.
Zh: 工业港口的大门紧锁着。你的后背感到一阵寒意。你像只动物一样抖动着，试着把水从皮毛上甩下来。

En: Clench your teeth to stop shuddering.
Zh: 咬紧牙关，让自己不再发抖。

En: Shake your shoulders again.
Zh: 再摇摇你的肩膀。

En: You shudder, looking down at your feet. Dirty rainwater runs veins into the plaza snow.
Zh: 你看着自己脚下，不住的颤抖着。肮脏的雨水在广场的雪地上蜿蜒流淌。

En: What's below?
Zh: 下面是什么？

En: Behind the gates -- heaps of supply crates. Red and blue metal shipping containers slick with rain. The Greater Revachol Industrial Harbour is an artificial mountain range. Immense wealth resides within, and immeasurable poverty in its shadow.
Zh: 大门下面——是一堆补给箱。红蓝相间的航运集装箱被雨水冲刷地滑溜溜的。瑞瓦肖大工业港是一座人造山脉，将巨大的财富蕴藏其中，也将不可估量的贫穷掩在了它的阴影之下。

En: And beyond that?
Zh: 在那上面呢？

En: And before that?
Zh: 在那前面呢？

En: You -- on the Martinaise plaza. A small dot looking up at the sky. Droplets form on your eyelashes.
Zh: 你——站在马丁内斯广场。就像一个凝望天空的小点。雨滴从你的睫毛上滑落下来。

En: La Drisienne, King Dris's Passenger Harbour. Cruise ships flanked by dock arms. Cranes watching over the mouth of the river distributary.
Zh: 德瑞斯利恩，德瑞斯国王的客运港口。游船两侧都是码头武装。起重机在河流的支流口上遥望。

En: What is across the distributary?
Zh: 支流的对岸是什么？

En: Couron, the lower middle class. Distributary after distributary cuts the city blocks in half. Seven-story buildings trail off into the rain.
Zh: 科戎，中下层阶级的聚居地。一道道支流将这座城市的街区切割成两半。七层楼的建筑逐渐消失在雨中。

En: A silvery curtain of rain over the houses. The class divide.
Zh: 银色的雨幕覆盖在房屋上，就此将阶层分割开来。

En: What is beyond the Couron?
Zh: 科戎上面是什么？

En: You have never been there. They don't need the law east of the river.
Zh: 你从没去过那里。河流的东岸不需要法律。

En: Sheets of rain over the water. A flight of stairs leading into the ocean. Wave after wave washing the coast of Martinaise, with its motorboats and gently swaying reeds.
Zh: 雨滴坠落在水面上。一段楼梯延伸入海。大海的波浪冲刷着马丁内斯海岸，还有那些停靠在岸边的摩托艇和微微轻摆的芦苇丛。

En: Capeside apartments -- tower blocks crowd one another, 4.46 mm bullets still lodged in their war-torn stone walls.
Zh: 岬岸公寓——高大的塔楼鳞次栉比，4.46毫米的子弹还卡在被战争摧毁的石墙上。

En: Will you ever go there?
Zh: 你会去那里吗？

En: What's above?
Zh: 上面是什么？

En: A traffic jam. Rain thrumming on the roofs of motor vehicles. Inside, drivers watch water streaming down their windshields. The statue of a king shudders, he too is cold. The canal bridge has been raised.
Zh: 大塞車。雨滴拍打在汽車顶上。車里，司机们看着雨水从挡风玻璃上汩汩地流淌下来。国王的雕像也在颤抖着，因为他也觉得太冷了。运河的大桥已经抬升起来了。

En: What's on the other side?
Zh: 另一边是什么？

En: The road ascends; a raised motorway loops above the ghetto. Beneath its concrete columns -- a sea of rooftops, woodwork, and tar stretches northward. Four-story buildings as far as the rain can fall. The snows melt in Jamrock.
Zh: 道路向上伸展着；高架高速公路在贫民区上方盘旋。水泥柱下方——是一片屋顶的海洋，木制品，还有焦油一路向北方延伸。雨滴所及之处，是四层楼的建筑。雪花在加姆洛克融化。

En: Motherfucker. [Finish thought.]
Zh: “狗娘养的。”[完成思维。]

En: These spring thaw will not last. The winter will return to Revachol.
Zh: 春季冰雪融化的季节不会持续太久。冬季很快会重新降临瑞瓦肖。

En: What is Jamrock?
Zh: 加姆洛克是什么？

En: Urban coastline, rain dripping off eternite-covered roofs. Cinder blocks left over from half-finished construction. A defunct research and development building once seized by revolutionaries. An old wooden church stands on stilts above the water.
Zh: 城市海岸线，雨水从亘石覆盖的屋顶滴落。半成品建筑的煤渣砖随意丢弃了一地。一栋曾经被革命者占领的废弃研究大楼。一座古老的教堂高高地矗立在海面上。

En: And beyond that?
Zh: 那上面呢？

En: Coal City, end of all lines.
Zh: 煤城，所有线路的终点。

En: Collapsed storm drains. Old sewage systems flooded with rainwater. Hidden weapon caches from the Revolution. Doors leading down to Le Royaume -- the catacombs to which, for three centuries, they delivered the blue-blooded dead.
Zh: 暴风雨铺天盖地。老旧的下水道系统被雨水淹没，将大革命时期遗留下来的秘密武器隐藏。向下方延伸的大门通向王国——三个多世纪以来，他们将贵族死者不断送往陵寝。

En: The skyscrapers of La Delta, the financial district. Faint golden light seeps from the office windows.
Zh: 金融区德尔塔的摩天大楼。办公室的窗户渗出微弱的金色光芒。

En: Who are you, ghosts?
Zh: 你是谁，幽灵？

En: What is down the shore?
Zh: 海岸下面是什么？

En: Run your fingers through your dampened hair.
Zh: 用手指拂过你湿漉漉的头发。

En: Your hair is an oily mess flecked with ash from neighbouring coal plants. Smoke stacks rise somewhere in the distance.
Zh: 你的头发油腻腻的，上面还沾着附近煤场吹出的点点飞灰。远处的某个地方，烟囱伸长了它的脖子。

En: Will I?
Zh: 我会吗？

En: Let go of the feeling.
Zh: 放开这种感觉。

En: No. You are just one of the hundreds of thousands who watch them rise across the bay from Martinaise every day.
Zh: 不。你只是千百万人中的一个，看着它们每天从马丁内斯海湾的对岸升起。

En: Your hair sticks to your forehead, cold and wet.
Zh: 你的头发贴在额头上，冰冷又潮湿。

En: Revachol is the capital of the world. Jamrock is the capital of Revachol. Droplets form on your eyelashes.
Zh: 瑞瓦肖是世界的中心。加姆洛克是瑞瓦肖的中心。雨水从你的睫毛上滴落下来。

En: It's home.
Zh: 这是家。

En: And closer to here?
Zh: 那这附近呢？

En: A yard. Rain falls onto the roof of a woodshed. Coal leaks into a puddle beneath the feet of a dead man. He swings from a tree, a gaping hole in his bare, rotting chest.
Zh: 一个院子。雨水从小木棚的屋顶上滑落下来。煤灰漏进死人脚下的一个水坑里。他挂在树上来回摇荡，赤裸腐烂的胸口有一个大洞。

En: A yard. Rain falls onto the roof of a woodshed. Coal leaks into a puddle beneath a dead man's feet. He swings from a tree, bloated. Droplets of rain slip from his cold cheeks.
Zh: 一个院子。雨水从小木棚的屋顶上滑落下来。煤灰漏进死人脚下的一个水坑里。他挂在树上来回摇荡，尸体已经有些膨胀。雨滴从他冰冷的脸颊滑过。

En: You realize you still have only one shoe. Nearby, on the balcony of the Whirling-in-Rags hostel-cafeteria, a single crocodile-skin shoe now fills with rainwater.
Zh: 你意识到自己只穿了一只鞋。褴褛飞旋旅社的阳台附近，一只鳄鱼皮鞋里积满了雨水。

En: Two green snakeskin shoes stand at attention on the mosaic paving of the plaza.
Zh: 两只绿色的蛇皮鞋立正站在广场地砖上。

En: You realize you have no shoes on. Your feet are red with cold.
Zh: 你意识到自己没穿鞋子，脚都被冻红了。

En: From the corner of his eye, the lieutenant gives your feet a worried glance. Rain drips from his glasses.
Zh: 警督眼角的余光透露出对你双脚的担忧。雨滴从他的眼镜上滑落下来。

En: It's cool. I'm in control.
Zh: “这样感觉挺酷的，一切都在我掌控之中。”

En: I should probably do something about this.
Zh: “也许我应该做点什么。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: The rain falls.
Zh: 雨水滑落。

En: The lieutenant nods, then looks east.
Zh: 警督点点头，然后朝东边看去。

En: The lieutenant nods, then looks east.
Zh: 警督点点头，然后朝东边看去。

En: What is there?
Zh: 那是什么？

En: Look up.
Zh: 向上看。

En: Why am I not there?
Zh: 为什么我不在那里？

En: What am I doing here?
Zh: 我在这里做什么？

En: Where do I live?
Zh: 我住在哪里？

En: To be in Martinaise, where no one goes. At the run-off point of a long-forgotten canal, in the whitest part of town. In the shadow of the day the Revolution failed.
Zh: 在马丁内斯，那是没有人去过的地方。在一条早已被人遗忘的运河径流口，也是整个镇子上最苍白的地方。在白昼的阴影下，大革命以失败告终。

En: On a street there that flows like a muddy river in the snow, with fire traps rising on either side. A film rental opens its doors to the rain, an armoured motor carriage rushes past the corner where you used to walk together... Suddenly, the hair on your back rises.
Zh: 街道宛如雪地流过的一弯泥泞河水，它的两边肃立着没有太平门的建筑。淅淅沥沥的雨中，一家电影租赁店还在营业，一辆装甲汽車从角落疾驰而过，那里就是你们曾经一起走过的地方……突然间，你感觉背上的汗毛全都竖了起来。

En: YOU CANNOT RETURN.
Zh: 你再也回不去了。

En: Standing in the rain, looking north, where Jamrock Rock City stretches inland.
Zh: 站在雨里，眺望北方，摇滚都市加姆洛克在那里向内陆延伸。

En: Where the hood, where the hood, where the hood at?
Zh: 我的猎犬在哪？我的豪车在哪？我的弟兄们在哪？

En: HAVE A BROTHER IN THE CUT. WHERE THE WOOD AT?
Zh: 哪里的大雨不停下？有人就在那陋巷里倒下。

En: Shudder, look further...
Zh: 浑身颤抖着，继续向远方眺望……

En: In the rain-swept distance above the rooftops of Jamrock, a re-purposed silk mill stands perched above the motorway exit. Precinct 41 hunches in the rain.
Zh: 远方一阵倾盆大雨，拍打着加姆洛克的屋顶，高速公路的出口旁坐落着一间改造过的丝绸厂。41分局在大雨中弓起了腰。

En: Satellite-Officer Jean Vicquemare rushes down the Precinct stairs, umbrella in hand. It's unopened. He doesn't seem pleased about the spring rain.
Zh: 随迁警官让·维克玛从辖区的楼梯上冲了下来，手里还拿着一把没有撑开的伞。他看上去好像不是很喜欢这场春雨。

En: On the bridge, Officers Torson and McLaine are standing guard. Torson wears jeans and a fishnet wifebeater. Satellite-Officer Vicquemare passes by, and the young man remarks to him...
Zh: 桥上，托森和麦克莱恩警官正在站岗。托森穿着牛仔裤和一件贴身鱼网背心。随迁警官维克玛经过，年轻人跟他搭起了话……

En: Where's your homo, homey?
Zh: “你的同性恋朋友去哪了，基佬？”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: It's not like that. They're what you call *hetero-sexual life partners*. They have a battle-tested relationship. A 'bröderbund', if you will.
Zh: “不是这样的。他们是大家口中的*异性恋生活伴侣*。彼此之间的关系久经考验。如果你喜欢的话，可以称之为‘兄弟情'。”

En: Huh? Yeah. He opens his umbrella, but the wind immediately turns it inside out.
Zh: “哈？是吗。”他打开雨伞，但却立即被狂风吹翻了过来。

En: Hetero -- sexual. Life -- partners.
Zh: “异性——恋。生活——伴侣。”

En: Funny apery. Vicquemare struggles with his umbrella, the ribs are protruding from its canopy. "Male-centric workplace humour. Have you seen him?"
Zh: “你这模仿的有点意思啊。”维克玛还在跟雨伞抗争，伞骨从雨篷中突了出来。“男性为主的工作场所专属的幽默。你见着他了吗？”

En: Is there something wrong?
Zh: “出什么问题了吗？”

En: No, nothing. It's just... Judit went to his place and found the Monday mail unopened. I think he's still *there*. You didn't, like, drink with him over the weekend did you?
Zh: “没，没什么。只不过……茱蒂特去他那看了看，发现周一的邮件没开过封。我觉得他应该还在*那里*。你不会……周末跟他一起喝酒去了吧？”

En: It's the only home you have now. But you can't go back. Not like this.
Zh: 现在这是你唯一的家了。但是你不能回去。这样可不行。

En: That would be *irresponsible*.
Zh: “那也*太不负责任*了。”

En: In Martinaise. He's in Martinaise.
Zh: “在马丁内斯。他就在马丁内斯。”

En: With that animal? Never again, man. What, is he still down there, in... you know, south of the interchange? The... what was it?
Zh: “跟那只野兽吗？再也不会了，伙计。什么，你说他还在那里，在……你知道的，枢纽站南边？那个……是什么来着？”

En: Your vision blurs. You wipe your face with your hand. The rain stings your eyes, making you look up and blink.
Zh: 你的视线变得模糊。你用手搓了搓脸。雨水刺痛了你的双眼，你不由得抬起头，眨了眨眼。

En: Coalition aerostatics hang like apparitions under the cloud cover. Way up there -- where rain forms -- rotors flutter silently. Your sight clears.
Zh: 联盟的静风舰就像幽灵一样悬挂在云层中。就在那上方——雨水形成的地方——转子静静地振动着。你的视线变得清晰起来。

En: This rain will not let up any time soon. You should get a raincoat. There's a Frittte to the east. They sell them there.
Zh: “这场雨不会很快就停的。你应该弄一件雨衣。东边有一间弗利多，那里有得卖。”

En: The ruins of a half-sunken seafort crumble on an inlet. Beyond the Bay of Revachol, ghosts rise into the sky.
Zh: 海湾入口的海堡早已崩塌瓦解，只剩废墟半沉在水中。瑞瓦肖的海湾之上，幽灵慢慢升往天空。

En: Hallways collapsed from the mortar hits of a war that was lost long ago. Clotheslines go to waste in the rain. Radios play.
Zh: 走廊早已在许久之前那场败仗的炮火洗礼下垮塌。晒衣绳在雨水中变成了废物。你听见收音机的声音。

En: This rain will not let up any time soon. At least we're dressed for it -- let's keep moving.
Zh: “这场雨不会很快就停的。不过至少我们身上的衣服还能扛住——继续前进吧。”

En: This rain will not let up any time soon. I'd recommend you put on your coat.
Zh: “这场雨不会很快就停的。我建议你穿上外套。”

En: The morning news.
Zh: 早间新闻。

En: The news at noon.
Zh: 午间新闻。

En: The evening news.
Zh: 晚间新闻。

En: Midnight news and reruns.
Zh: 午夜新闻和重播节目。

En: Your shirt sticks to your chest. The shoulders of your jacket grow heavy. The cold finds its way under your skin. You shiver, and the city shivers with you.
Zh: 衬衫紧贴在你的胸口。夹克的肩部变得沉重。寒冷一路钻到你的皮肤之下。你颤抖着，整个城市也跟着你一起颤抖。

En: Your shirt sticks to your chest. The cold finds its way under your skin. You shiver, and the city shivers with you.
Zh: 衬衫紧贴在你的胸口。寒冷一路钻到你的皮肤之下。你颤抖着，整个城市也跟着你一起颤抖。

En: The shoulders of your jacket grow heavy. The cold finds its way under your skin. You shiver, and the city shivers with you.
Zh: 夹克的肩部变得沉重。寒冷一路钻到你的皮肤之下。你颤抖着，整个城市也跟着你一起颤抖。

En: Water runs down your bare chest. The cold finds its way under your skin. You shiver, and the city shivers with you.
Zh: 雨水沿着你赤裸的胸膛滑落。寒冷一路钻到你的皮肤之下。你颤抖着，整个城市也跟着你一起颤抖。

En: Humid. Your coat shields you from the rain while the city shivers around you.
Zh: 潮湿。外套帮你挡住了雨水，周围的整座城市都在随你一起颤抖。

En: Winter's grip on the city is loosening. The spring thaw is here.
Zh: 冬季放松了对这座城市的掌控。春融期已经到来。

En: Finally. What now?
Zh: 总算好了。现在呢？

En: A yard. Rain falls onto the roof of a woodshed. Filthy water pools around a body. Droplets of rain slip from the dead man's cold cheeks.
Zh: 一个院子。雨水从小木棚的屋顶上滑落下来。脏水在尸体旁汇成一弯水池。雨滴从死人冰冷的脸颊上滑过。

En: A yard. Rain falls onto the roof of a woodshed. The lingering odour of decomposition mixes with that of damp soil.
Zh: 一个院子。雨水从小木棚的屋顶上滑落下来。脏水在尸体旁汇成一弯水池。腐烂迟迟不散的味道跟潮湿的泥土味混合在了一起。

En: More coalition aerostatics. Way up there -- where rain forms -- rotors flutter silently. Your sight clears.
Zh: 更多联盟的静风舰。就在那上方——雨水形成的地方——转子静静地振动着。你的视线变得清晰起来。

En: Patrol Officer Judit Minot stands beneath the awning overhanging the entrance, struggling to open her big black umbrella.
Zh: 巡警茱蒂特·迈诺特站在入口悬挂的雨篷下，挣扎着想要撑开自己的黑色雨伞。

En: The Precinct surgeon, chewing on a piece of candy, throws her a questioning glance, walking by: "Need a hand with that?"
Zh: 分局的外科医生嘴里咀嚼着一颗糖果，路过的时候向她投去了疑问的一瞥：“需要帮忙吗？”

En: No. Thank you. I got it. She gets the umbrella to open, and, with a quick smile and nod to the surgeon, sprints down the steps. The rain falls all around her...
Zh: “不用了，谢谢。我自己能行。”她撑开雨伞，笑着朝医生点点头，随后快步冲下了台阶。雨水降落在她的周围……

En: Wonder if Vic's found his long lost boyfriend yet. He looks over at Chester McLaine and breaks into a laugh at his own joke. The rain falls outside.
Zh: “不知道维克有没有找到他那个失踪很久的男朋友。”他看着切斯特·麦克莱恩，突然被自己的话给逗笑了。雨在外面淅淅沥沥地下着。

En: Mack, they're *hetero-sexual life partners*. It's not like that, his partner smirks. "But yeah. There's trouble in paradise for that duo, Tequila Sunset has..." The sound of the rain grows so loud it drowns out his voice.
Zh: “老兄，他们是*异性恋生活伴侣*。不是那样的，”他的同伴假笑到。“不过也是。那种双人组的天堂里也是有麻烦的，龙舌兰日落总是……”雨声越来越大，把他的声音渐渐淹没。

En: Wait -- your old friend has prepared you for this -- at great cost to himself. Make sure you have the spirit bomb in your hand when *shit goes south*.
Zh: 等等——你的老朋友帮你准备好了——本人还为此付出了极大的代价。当等*情况变坏*的时候，记得手里一定要准备好酒精炸弹。

En: Wait -- your old friend has prepared you for this -- at great cost to himself. Make sure you have the spirit bomb in your hand when *shit goes south*.
Zh: 等等——你的老朋友帮你准备好了——本人还为此付出了极大的代价。等*情况变坏*的时候，记得手里一定要准备好酒精炸弹。

En: For God's sake -- find some bullets before you go any further. An unloaded gun won't do you any good!
Zh: 看在老天爷的份上——继续前进之前先找点子弹吧。没上膛的枪也没什么大用啊！

En: For God's sake -- find some bullets before you go any further. An unloaded gun won't do you any good!
Zh: 看在老天爷的份上——继续前进之前先找点子弹吧。没上膛的枪也没什么大用啊！

En: For God's sake -- find some bullets before you go any further. An unloaded gun won't do you any good!
Zh: 看在老天爷的份上——继续前进之前先找点子弹吧。没上膛的枪也没什么大用啊！

En: Stop. Now. It is time.
Zh: 停。就是现在。时间到了。

En: Yes. And it's not going to be fun.
Zh: 是的。而且一点都不会好玩。

En: Time for the tribunal?
Zh: 审判时间到了？

En: Time to party?
Zh: 派对时间到了？

En: Time for grim, serious, deadly, dangerous. It's not going to be fun.
Zh: 残酷、严肃、致命、危险的时间到了。而且一点都不会好玩。

En: What am I supposed to do?
Zh: 我应该做什么？

En: Be prepared. Make sure you have your pepperbox in your hand.
Zh: 做好准备。保证手里握着你的转管手枪。

En: Your old friend has prepared you for this -- at great cost to himself. Make sure you have the spirit bomb in your hand when *shit goes south*.
Zh: 你的老朋友已经为你准备好了——他付出了非常大的代价。等*情况变坏*的时候，记得手里一定要准备好酒精炸弹。

En: Be prepared. Wield Ruby's double-barrelled pistol.
Zh: 做好准备。用上露比的双管手枪。

En: What you have isn't enough. You'll need more firepower. A ranged weapon.
Zh: 你现有的东西还不够。你需要更强的火力。一只远程武器。

En: Stop. Just up ahead: danger.
Zh: 站住。前方等待你的是：危险。

En: You are prepared. Don't put away your friend. Your weapon.
Zh: 你已经准备好了。不要抛弃你的朋友。你的武器。

En: Raindrops slip from the barrels of your Villiers 9mm pistol.
Zh: 雨点从你的维利耶9毫米手枪的枪管上滑落。

En: Your fingers reflexively reach for the Villiers 9mm pistol in your pocket.
Zh: 你的手指反射性地扣住了口袋里的维利耶9毫米手枪。

En: You hear what sounds like an enthusiastic gurgling coming from the spirit bomb in your pocket.
Zh: 你听见口袋里酒精炸弹的声音，它好像正在狂热的大笑。

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: Sounds ominous.
Zh: 听起来不太吉利啊。

En: For what?
Zh: 什么时间？

En: Crisis, death. You can feel it in your blue soul.
Zh: 危机，死亡。你可以在自己蓝色的灵魂中感觉到它。

En: It is.
Zh: 确实是。

En: No, no. You cannot get off that easy. This is serious. Lives are at stake.
Zh: 不不。你不可能这么简单就动身。这很严肃。生命危在旦夕。

En: What a shitty gun. But it's better than nothing.
Zh: 这枪也太次了。不过总比没有的好。

En: Yours included.
Zh: 还包括你的。

En: Arm yourself with anything you can. Unless you have... nothing? Because you didn't find your lost gun? Then just walk to your death.
Zh: 想尽一切办法武装自己。除非你……什么也没有？因为你没找到丢失的枪？那你就准备踏入死亡之路吧。

En: Kim, there's danger. Up ahead.
Zh: “金，我感觉到危险。就在前面。”

En: Yes. I hear commotion... He cups his ear. "Let's go."
Zh: “是的。我听见了骚乱的声音……”他竖起耳朵。“我们走吧。”

En: You know what you should do? Take out the spirit bomb...
Zh: 你知道自己应该做什么吗？把酒精炸弹拿出来……

En: Shush. He's not drinking the spirit bomb. He's putting it in his hand and he's going to be ready.
Zh: 嘘。他没有喝掉酒精炸弹。他把它放在手里，然后就准备好了。

En: I can talk you out of it, whatever it is.
Zh: 不管是什么，我都可以说服你的。

En: You're too likable to die. Don't worry.
Zh: 你这么讨人喜欢，不会死的。别担心。

En: Stop. Just up ahead: danger.
Zh: 站住。前方等待你的是：危险。

En: You are prepared. Don't put away your friend. Your weapon. It is glowing in your hand, ready to serve.
Zh: 你准备好了。不要抛弃你的朋友。你的武器。它在你的手里闪闪发光，随时准备为你服务。

En: Raindrops slip down the oily fabric of the tail end of your necktie-turned-fuse.
Zh: 雨滴降落在你那条油腻领带引线的末端。

En: Snow gathers on the oily fabric of the tail end of your necktie-turned-fuse.
Zh: 雨滴降落在你那条油腻领带引线的末端。

En: The oily tail end of your necktie-turned-fuse glistens in the cool spring light.
Zh: 你那条油腻领带引线末端在寒冷的春光中闪耀。

En: Snowflakes speck the barrels of your Villiers 9mm pistol.
Zh: 雪花斑驳地飘落在你的维利耶9毫米手枪的枪管上。

En: The barrels of your Villiers 9mm pistol shine threateningly in the cold spring light.
Zh: 维利耶9毫米手枪的枪管在寒冷的春光中闪烁着威胁性的光芒。

En: Stop. Just up ahead: danger.
Zh: 站住。前方等待你的是：危险。

En: You are prepared. Don't put away your friend. Your weapon.
Zh: 你准备好了。不要抛弃你的朋友。还有你的武器。

En: Snowflakes speck the barrels of your Nachtwey A80 pistol.
Zh: 雪花斑驳地飘落在你的纳克特威A80手枪的枪管上。

En: The barrels of your Nachtwey A80 pistol shine threateningly in the cold spring light.
Zh: 纳克特威A80手枪的枪管在寒冷的春光中闪烁着威胁性的光芒。

En: Raindrops slip from the barrels of your Nachtwey A80 pistol.
Zh: 雨滴降落在你的纳克特威A80手枪的枪管上。

En: Why don't you take out your trusty Villiers instead?
Zh: 为什么不拿出你最信赖的维利耶呢？

En: There really isn't much of a difference between these two guns...
Zh: 这两把枪真的没多大区别……

En: What?! You can't compare the sidearm issued to you personally by the RCM to the pistol of a known criminal?
Zh: 什么？！你不能拿RCM发给你个人的武器跟一个知名罪犯的手枪做比较吧？

En: I'm ready to do this.
Zh: 我已经准备好了。

En: I'll feel prepared as soon as I switch out my real police gun.
Zh: 当我掏出真正的警枪时，就已经感觉准备好了。

En: Good. Be ready to take damage.
Zh: 很好。那就准备承受伤害吧。

En: Do what you need to do. And then keep moving toward the future.
Zh: 做你需要做的事吧。然后继续朝着未来前进。

En: Stop. Just up ahead: danger.
Zh: 站住。前方等待你的是：危险。

En: Water streams down your Kvalsund KR+2 Multi-Tool. It looks impressive -- but what can it do against military-grade weaponry?
Zh: 雨水从你的库瓦桑德KR+2多用工具上淌下。它是让人印象深刻——但是怎么可能对抗军事级武器呢？

En: Water streams down your prybar. Or crowbar. Or prybar. Call it what you will -- it doesn't stand a chance against military-grade weaponry.
Zh: 雨水从你的撬棍上淌下。或者是铁撬。或者叫撬棒也行。随你怎么称呼——但它在军事级武器面前毫无胜算。

En: Statistically speaking, sabres have proven to be rather ineffective against  military technology.
Zh: 从统计学的角度来讲，军刀已经被证实完全无法有效对抗军事技术。

En: Snowflakes stick to your Kvalsund KR+2 Multi-Tool. It looks impressive -- but what can it do against military-grade weaponry?
Zh: 雪花附着在你的库瓦桑德KR+2多用工具上。它是让人印象深刻——但是怎么可能对抗军事级武器呢？

En: Snowflakes stick to your prybar. Or crowbar. Or prybar. Call it what you will -- it doesn't stand a chance against military-grade weaponry.
Zh: 雪花附着在你的撬棍上。或者是铁撬。或者叫撬棒也行。随你怎么称呼——但它在军事级武器面前毫无胜算。

En: Statistically speaking, sabres have proven to be rather ineffective against advanced military technology.
Zh: 从统计学的角度来讲，军刀已经被证实完全无法有效对抗先进的军事技术。

En: Your Kvalsund KR+2 Multi-Tool looks impressive, shining in the cold spring light -- but what can it do against military-grade weaponry?
Zh: 你的库瓦桑德KR+2多用工具在寒冷的春光下闪闪发亮，给人留下了深刻的印象——但是它能怎么对抗军事级武器呢？

En: You try to take comfort in the weight of your prybar. Or crowbar. Or prybar. Call it what you will -- it doesn't stand a chance against military-grade weaponry.
Zh: 你试着从手中撬棍的重量上寻求安慰。或者是铁撬。或者叫撬棒也行。随你怎么称呼——但它在军事级武器面前毫无胜算。

En: Statistically speaking, sabres have proven to be rather ineffective against advanced military technology.
Zh: 从统计学的角度来讲，军刀已经被证实完全无法有效对抗先进的军事技术。

En: I prefer hand-to-hand combat. It's more honourable.
Zh: “我更喜欢徒手格斗。这样更光荣一些。”

En: I prefer hand-to-hand combat.
Zh: 我更喜欢徒手格斗。

En: I'm not sure I feel ready for what lies ahead.
Zh: “我不确定有没有准备好面对前方的一切。”

En: And you think you're off to die an honourable death? What about the lives of the people depending on you?
Zh: 那你是想光荣的死去了？那依靠你的那些人呢，他们的性命怎么办？

En: This is not the time to discuss preferences. It's not just your own life that will be at stake.
Zh: 没时间讨论什么偏好了。不仅仅是你一个人的生命危在旦夕。

En: Then you'd better get ready. He frowns, then quickly adds, "Whatever happens, I've got your back."
Zh: 警督皱了皱眉。“那你最好做好准备，”他飞快地补充到。“不论发生什么，我都在你背后。”

En: I'll fight with my bare hands if I have to.
Zh: 如果有必要的话，我就用两只手战斗。

En: It would be better if you didn't have to. It's not just your own life that will be at stake.
Zh: 如果不用这样会更好一些。不仅仅是你一个人的生命危在旦夕。

En: I'm scared...
Zh: 我害怕……

En: This isn't the time for fear. Go.
Zh: 现在没时间害怕。走吧。

En: There! Working class drunk! You know what this means, right?
Zh: 看啦！工人阶级醉汉！你应该知道这是什么意思，对吧？

En: Who's Dick Mullen now?
Zh: 迪克·马伦又是谁啊？

En: [Discard thought.]
Zh: [放弃思维。]

En: Case solved!
Zh: 案子解决了！

En: I am.
Zh: 是我。

En: Cracked it. All in a good day's work.
Zh: 全部搞定。只用好好工作一天就够了。

En: Wait, what did I crack exactly?
Zh: 等等，我到底搞定什么了？

En: Open and shut. I don't even need to think about this anymore. Goodbye. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 太明显了。我甚至都不用再想一遍。再见。[完成思维。]

En: What do you mean *what did I crack*? Look at how working class that drunk is!
Zh: 你说*我搞定什么了*是什么意思？看看那个醉汉有多工人阶级？

En: It's her husband! The missing husband!
Zh: 那是她的丈夫！那个不见的丈夫！

En: It's the future leader of a proletarian uprising.
Zh: 那是工人阶级起义的未来领袖。

En: It's... uhm... some third option?
Zh: 那是……呃……第三个选项？

En: No. Stop goofing around, recruit!
Zh: 不是。不要再搞些乱七八糟的了，新人！

En: No, it's not.
Zh: 不，不是的。

En: Yes. And you found him. Now go and tell the working class woman. Protect and serve, recruit!
Zh: 是的。而且你发现他了。现在去告诉那个女工人吧。保护市民，服务市民，新人！

En: Didn't she repeatedly tell you her husband isn't missing though?
Zh: 她不是再三告诉你自己的丈夫没有失踪吗？

En: But he *is* missing. You heard her, the worry in her voice.
Zh: 但他*确实*失踪了啊。你也听到她说了，口气那么担心。

En: Do marriages make any sense? Does honour? You're not a filthy philosopher, you're an officer of law. It's time to *ace this case* and not brood over your reputation. We can deal with the perception management later.
Zh: 婚姻有意义吗？很光荣呢？你不是一个肮脏的哲学家，你是一名执法人员。是时候*把案子放在第一位*了，不要太计较自己的名声。我们可以之后再处理知觉管理的问题。

En: Not honourable, recruit!! Not honourable at all.
Zh: 没什么好光荣的，新人！一点都不光荣。

En: This is non-sense. [Discard the thought.]
Zh: 这都是废话。[放弃思维。]

En: Give me champagne! I'm going *in*.
Zh: 给我来杯香槟！我要*加入*。

En: This doesn't add up. I'm only making a fool of myself.
Zh: 这样说不通。我只会让自己出洋相的。

En: There's no need for champagne when there's honour, recruit. Go and tell the working class woman what you found *right now*.
Zh: 如果有荣誉的话，就不需要香槟了，新人。*马上*把你的发现告诉女工人吧。
